title: Re-check safety subset against standard updates
context: Safety signs and hazard communication standards (ISO 7010, ISO 7001, GHS) are revised periodically. Any revision that changes the required meaning or visual convention of a concept referenced by an open-pictograms safety-aligned symbol could cause harm if the symbol is not updated. This maintenance task schedules a periodic re-check of the safety subset against current standards, triggering redesign-and-retest or withdrawal for any symbol whose concept alignment is affected.
objective: Re-check all safety-aligned symbols against the current versions of referenced standards (ISO 7010, ISO 7001, GHS, AIGA/DOT); identify any symbols whose concept alignment is affected; trigger redesign-and-retest or withdrawal as appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: Review covers all safety-aligned symbols in the set; compares each against the current version of its referencedStandard (ISO 7010, ISO 7001, GHS, AIGA/DOT) and flags any change in required meaning, recommended visual convention, or concept scope.

For any flagged symbol: triggers a redesign-and-retest cycle (meeting the stricter ≥85% correct + <5% critical confusion threshold) or formal withdrawal from the shipped set; no out-of-date safety symbol remains in the released set.

Domain-expert sign-off is re-obtained for any redesigned safety symbol before it is re-released; the 'not certified safety signage / not medical advice' disclaimer is re-confirmed on all safety-aligned symbols.
Re-check findings are documented in a maintenance report (including: standards versions reviewed, symbols affected, actions taken, sign-off records); published in docs/maintenance/.
Maintenance report is versioned and licensed CC-BY-4.0; any symbol metadata updated to reflect the new review date.
resources: ISO 7010 (safety signs — concepts only, standard not redistributed)
output: docs/maintenance/safety-subset-recheck-<date>.md — a maintenance report documenting standards versions reviewed, symbols flagged, redesign/withdrawal actions taken, and expert sign-offs; updated metadata records; licensed CC-BY-4.0.
